mission.title: kubevirt: Live Migration Fails to Select Compatible Target Node Based on Machine Type
mission.description: Live Migration Fails to Select Compatible Target Node Based on Machine Type. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kubevirt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubevirt deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
**What happened**:
During the live migration of a VM, if the target node does not support the VM's machine type, the migration will fail, even if other nodes support the VM's original machine type.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Live Migration Fails to Select Compatible Target Node Based…
steps.description:
Before this PR:
Nodes labeled by the node labeller did not include supported machine types. As a result, a Virtual Machine (VM) with a specific machine type could be scheduled on a node that does not support it. This would cause the VM launcher to fail.

After this PR:
* Nodes are now labeled by the node-labeller with all supported machine types available on each node.
* When `virt-laucnher` pods being created during vmi starts or during migration, they will have a label in the `NodeSelector`

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Live Migration Fails to Select Compatible Target… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Before this PR:
Nodes labeled by the node labeller did not include supported machine types. As a result, a Virtual Machine (VM) with a specific machine type could be scheduled on a node that does not support it. This would cause the VM launcher to fail.

After this PR:
* Nodes are now labeled by the node-labeller with all supported machine types available on each node.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
